overview.service: Direct-to-consumer telehealth and prescription services for sexual health, mental health, dermatology, and weight management
overview.consumer_base: Adults in the US seeking accessible, stigma-reduced healthcare for sensitive conditions
overview.partnership_rationale: Hims & Hers has built one of the largest direct-to-consumer telehealth platforms for traditionally stigmatized conditions, generating longitudinal data on patient-initiated care seeking, treatment adherence, and health outcomes that align with UNC's behavioral health, sexual health, and health equity research.
overview.primary_contact: Hims & Hers Clinical and Research Partnerships
overview.contact_reasoning: Hims & Hers has collaborated with academic researchers on telehealth access and utilization studies, and its focus on underserved care categories creates research alignment with UNC's health equity and behavioral health programs.

basic_info.name_and_location: Hims & Hers Health Inc., San Francisco, CA

unc_connection.donation_history: No confirmed direct donation history to UNC on record. Verify via UNC Office of Development.

talking_points.detail: Hims & Hers serves over 2 million subscribers across sexual health, mental health, dermatology, and weight management, generating longitudinal data on patient-initiated care seeking for conditions where traditional care access is limited by stigma or geography.

Hims & Hers launched a compounded semaglutide and branded GLP-1 program, rapidly becoming one of the largest direct-to-consumer obesity medicine providers — creating research opportunities on medication adherence, outcomes, and patient experience in DTC pharmacy models.

Hims & Hers provides online psychiatry and therapy services for anxiety and depression at low cost and without insurance requirements, generating research data on digital mental health access, treatment initiation, and engagement across demographics.

talking_points.topic: Stigma Reduction and Care Initiation Research
talking_points.detail: The DTC model removes traditional barriers to care initiation for stigmatized conditions, creating a natural experiment for UNC researchers studying how access modality affects care-seeking behavior and health outcomes in mental and sexual health.
Hims & Hers operates its own licensed pharmacy and generates prescription fulfillment and adherence data that is applicable to UNC's pharmacy informatics and medication adherence research programs.

references.title: Direct-to-Consumer Telehealth: Implications for Access and Quality
